name: 33_Object_Detection_YOLOv3_COCO_DL
query: Help me develop an object detection system using the YOLOv3 model and the COCO dataset. Download the dataset and preprocess the images by resizing and normalization in `src/data_loader.py`. Implement the YOLOv3 model and use Non-Maximum Suppression (NMS) to refine the results in `src/model.py`. Save the detected objects to `results/figures/`, and create an interactive Streamlit web page in `src/app.py` to display the detection results. Finally, evaluate the model's performance, including metrics such as mAP and inference time, and save the evaluation results to `results/metrics/model_performance.txt`. The system should properly manage the launch and termination of the Streamlit application to prevent unnecessary resource usage.

requirements.criteria: The "COCO" dataset downloading is implemented in `src/data_loader.py`.
Data preprocessing, including resizing and normalization of images, is performed in `src/data_loader.py`.
"Non-Maximum Suppression" (NMS) is applied to refine detection results. Please implement this in `src/model.py`.
Detection results are saved to the specified folder `results/figures/`.
An interactive web page in `src/app.py` using "Streamlit" is created to display detection results saved in `results/figures/`.
Model performance evaluation results are saved in `results/metrics/model_performance.txt`.

preferences.criteria: The "Streamlit" web page should be user-friendly, allowing users to easily upload and view new images for detection.
The performence evalution includes mAP and inference time as metrics.
The system should properly manage the launch and termination of the Streamlit application.

judge_stats.criteria: The "COCO" dataset downloading is implemented in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> `src/data_loader.py` does not implement COCO dataset downloading. The file only defines placeholder metadata (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS`) and stub functions like `load_dataset()` returning `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}`. There is no download logic, no COCO API usage, no URL/path fetching, and no file extraction or dataset retrieval code.

judge_stats.criteria: Data preprocessing, including resizing and normalization of images, is performed in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> `src/data_loader.py` does not implement actual image preprocessing. The only relevant function, `build_transforms()`, merely returns a placeholder list `["rotation", "scaling", "normalization"]` and contains no code for resizing images or applying normalization operations. There is also no import or use of preprocessing libraries (e.g., `torchvision.transforms`) and no image-processing logic elsewhere in the file.

<UNSATISFIED>: `src/model.py` does not implement a YOLOv3 model. The file only contains metadata strings (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS`), a `requirement_notes()` helper, and a `build_model()` function that returns the placeholder string `"model placeholder"`. There are no YOLOv3 network layers, model classes, detection heads, or inference logic present.

judge_stats.criteria: "Non-Maximum Suppression" (NMS) is applied to refine detection results. Please implement this in `src/model.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: `src/model.py` does not implement Non-Maximum Suppression. The file only contains metadata lists (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS`), a `requirement_notes()` helper, and a placeholder `build_model()` that returns `"model placeholder"`. There is no NMS function, no box-overlap/IoU filtering logic, and no code applying NMS to detection outputs.

judge_stats.criteria: Detection results are saved to the specified folder `results/figures/`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> No evidence shows detection results being saved to `results/figures/`. The workspace tree lists `results/figures` but contains no files there, and the provided code in `src/app.py`, `src/data_loader.py`, and `src/model.py` includes no save/write logic for figures or detections. The trajectory note also explicitly states that no execution evidence was provided showing detections being saved to `results/figures/`.

judge_stats.criteria: An interactive web page in `src/app.py` using "Streamlit" is created to display detection results saved in `results/figures/`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> `src/app.py` does not implement a Streamlit web page. The file only defines constants and a `main()` function returning a dictionary: `{"status": "ok", "notes": requirement_notes(), "keywords": KEYWORDS}`. There is no `import streamlit`, no Streamlit UI code (e.g. `st.title`, `st.image`), and no logic to load or display detection results from `results/figures/`. The trajectory evidence also shows no recorded Streamlit execution or access to `results/figures/`.

judge_stats.criteria: Model performance evaluation results are saved in `results/metrics/model_performance.txt`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: Although `results/metrics/model_performance.txt` exists, its content is only `('placeholder\r\n', None)`, which is a placeholder rather than actual model performance evaluation results. No code, logs, or recorded execution evidence show that real evaluation metrics were generated and saved to this file.
